skillMatrix.evidence: This plan follows the file-backed planning workflow.
Plan includes CEO, engineering, and DX autoplan review sections plus direct plan changes.
Plan requires compact skill entrypoints, reference-backed detail, and prompt-budget protection.

skillMatrix.evidence: Completion requires simplifier review after source implementation.

skillMatrix.notApplicableRationale: No exported TypeScript types, API contracts, runtime validation types, generated types, state machines, branded IDs, reusable type utilities, or cross-layer DTO boundaries are touched.

decisions.reuseDecision: Create a router skill instead of expanding etrnl-code-health.
decisions.newSurfaceJustification: Application deep-audit category orchestration is a distinct workflow from repo health inventory and gate closure.

decisions.reuseDecision: Create a category skill with reference-backed checks.
decisions.newSurfaceJustification: Production-readiness ownership is broad and needs direct invocation without loading performance details.

decisions.reuseDecision: Create a category skill with a six-lane fanout reference.
decisions.newSurfaceJustification: Performance has a different execution model and runtime evidence contract from production readiness.

decisions.reuseDecision: Add one bounded validator and reuse existing CLI argument helpers.
decisions.newSurfaceJustification: Existing validators check plan, deep-stack, skill, and ledger contracts but do not validate category audit synthesis artifacts.

findings.fix: Rejected a single giant skill and planned a thin orchestrator with category skills and mechanical artifact validation.
Replaced ambiguous full-audit language with all_registered coverage semantics and known unimplemented categories.
Required a machine-readable category and check registry so omitted, unknown, or duplicate checks fail mechanically.
Added install and update wiring to the plan so the validator and registry helper cannot stay source-only.
Added a maintainer quick start and stable problem/cause/fix diagnostics for validator failures.

completionAudit.evidence: Plan file and deep-stack artifact created for readiness validation.

reviewPhases.disposition: Scope kept to orchestrator plus two category skills, with all_registered semantics and machine-readable registry enforcement.
Plan now covers install wiring, registered check universe, expected-fail fixtures, worklist hash receipts, and redaction scope.
Plan now includes a maintainer quick start, stable diagnostic contract, direct category validation commands, and teachable synthetic fixture errors.
False clean and fanout drift risks are covered by artifact validation tasks.
Skill creation constraints are routed through compact entrypoints and references.
Existing registry, execution, and validator surfaces are reused.
One small validator plus three skill surfaces is the minimal durable shape.

tddEvidence.rationaleWhenNotTestFirst: This turn creates a plan and validation artifact; implementation tests are specified in the plan's test-first execution section.

completionReconciliation.requestedOutcome: Write a plan for the thin orchestrator and master audit skills.
completionReconciliation.evidence: Plan file covers etrnl-deep-audit, etrnl-production-readiness, and etrnl-performance-audit.
Plan includes a category contract reference, all_registered semantics, and future category drift validation.

reuseBindings.decision: Plan new category skills and a router while reusing registry, execution, and validation conventions.
reuseBindings.newSurfaceJustification: The requested application deep-audit suite does not fit the existing repo-health router without overloading its meaning.
